name: Tech Pulse
description: Builder intelligence briefing: scans Twitter/X, Exa, HN, and GitHub for signals about YC startups, funding, emerging techniques, and ecosystem shifts. Outputs a ranked text briefing + 2-segment ElevenLabs audio narration. ~$0.06/run, no API keys required.

data.label: Tech Pulse
data.name: Tech Pulse
data.description: Twitter-first tech intelligence briefing. Surfaces signals about where the ecosystem is heading: YC-funded startups, funding patterns, technique discoveries, emerging paradigms, and what builders are collectively moving toward. Outputs a ranked text briefing + audio narration. No API keys — all sources paid via x402 USDC.
data.useCases: Use when the user runs /tech-pulse, asks for a tech briefing, wants to know what's getting funded or built, or asks what's trending in AI/developer tools. Accepts an optional topic — defaults to broad AI/developer-tool signals.
data.notFor: Not for general news (use /headlines), crypto prices (use /last30days-crypto), or individual tool reviews (use /tools-pulse). Not for topics requiring live TV or broadcast data.

data.label: Topic (optional)
data.description: The tech topic to scan for signals. Leave blank for a broad AI/developer-tools briefing. Examples: 'knowledge graphs', 'YC', 'agent memory', 'LLM inference'.
data.placeholder: AI developer tools startups funding trends 2026

data.label: X/Twitter: Tech Signals
data.description: Search Twitter/X for high-engagement posts in the last 14 days about the topic. Focus on: ecosystem trends (builders moving from X to Y), technique discoveries, paradigm shifts, and what VCs or prominent builders are publicly excited about. For each result capture: handle, URL, summary, like count, and what it signals about where the ecosystem is heading.

data.label: X/Twitter: YC & Funding Signals
data.description: Search Twitter/X for the last 14 days of posts about YC batch launches, seed and Series A funding announcements for developer-tool companies, and what top VCs are publicly excited about. Include posts from @garrytan, @paulg, @ycombinator, and founders announcing launches. For each: handle, URL, summary, likes.

data.label: Exa: Startup & Funding News
data.description: Neural search for articles published in the last 21 days about the topic — focusing on startup launches, seed/Series A funding rounds, and YC activity. Prefer results from TechCrunch, a16z blog, YC blog, and reputable tech publications. Return title, URL, publish date, and a 300-char excerpt.

startPublishedDate.description: ISO date to filter results after (YYYY-MM-DD)

data.label: Exa: Techniques & Research
data.description: Neural search for blog posts, research papers, and technical articles from the last 21 days about the topic — focusing on emerging techniques, paradigm shifts, and research with practical builder implications. Summarize the key implication for builders, not the full paper. Return title, URL, publish date, and 300-char excerpt.

startPublishedDate.description: ISO date to filter results after (YYYY-MM-DD)

data.label: GitHub: Trending Repos
data.description: Search GitHub for newly created repositories (last 14 days) in the topic area that have gained significant stars quickly. High star velocity on new repos = strong trend signal. For each repo: full_name, star count, description, topics. What categories are these repos in?

type.description: Type of search: repositories, code, issues

data.label: HN: Community Discussion (Free)
Filter, Score & Deduplicate
data.instruction:
Combine all research results (Twitter x2, Exa x2, GitHub, HN) and apply the quality filter and scoring system.

KEEP if it is a genuine signal:
- YC startup launch or seed/Series A funding announcement
- Technique/method that could change how builders work
- Trend with data: 'builders moving from X to Y'
- Research paper with practical builder implication
- What VCs or prominent builders are publicly excited about
- Paradigm shift being debated on X / HN

REJECT:
- A specific tool to install (that is /tools-pulse)
- Opinion without evidence or data point  
- Corporate press release (blog post, conference recap)
- Duplicate of another item in this run
- Paywalled content with no usable summary

SCORING — add points for each signal:
- X likes ≥ 200 on a trend/startup post: +4
- X likes 50–199: +2 | X likes 10–49: +1
- HN ≥ 100 pts on trend discussion: +4
- HN 30–99 pts: +2
- Exa result from known publication (TechCrunch, a16z, YC blog): +2
- GitHub repo >100 stars in 14 days in trend-relevant category: +3
- Confirmed YC-backed or seed/Series A funded: +3
- Multiple sources pointing to same trend: +3
- Published in last 7 days: +1

Return top 5–8 items ranked by score. If fewer than 3 unique signals, flag that explicitly.

data.label: Write Text Briefing
data.instruction:
Format the filtered, scored signals into a clean text briefing. Group into sections only if 2+ items share a category; otherwise use a flat numbered list.

Sections: YC & Funding | Techniques | Ecosystem Shifts

Format per item:
1. **{Title}**
   {Sentence 1: what it is / what happened.} {Sentence 2: why this matters as a signal — what it reveals about where things are heading.}
   Source: {source label with engagement metric if available}

RULES:
- Always say WHY something matters. Not just 'X startup launched' but 'X launched — this is the third YC company this month betting on [category], which tells you where the funding is clustering.'
- 2 sentences max per item. No raw URLs in the briefing text.
- End with a one-line source summary: 'X ({N} posts) | Exa ({N} articles) | HN ({N} stories, top signal: {top title} {N} pts) | GitHub ({N} repos) | {N} new signals'

This briefing is shown directly to the user. Make it sharp and opinionated.

data.label: Write Audio Narration Script
data.instruction:
Write a broadcast narration script split into exactly TWO segments. Each segment MUST be ≤140 words (hard limit — the TTS endpoint rejects more). Count every word.

SEGMENT 1 — Intro + top signals (~140 words):
- Open: 'Builder intelligence briefing, {weekday} {Month Day}. Here's what's moving in the ecosystem.'
- Cover top 2–3 signals: one sentence each on WHAT and WHY it matters
- End: 'More signals coming up.'

SEGMENT 2 — Remaining signals + close (~140 words):
- Cover remaining 2–3 signals
- Close: 'That's your tech pulse for today. Tomorrow's signals load fresh.'

SCRIPT RULES: active voice, present tense, no URLs, no @handles, no markdown. Spell out numbers ('three thousand likes' not '3,000'). Trim to 140 words if over — remove the least important clause first.

Output SEGMENT_1 and SEGMENT_2 as two clearly labeled text blocks.

data.label: ElevenLabs TTS: Segment 1
data.description: Convert SEGMENT_1 of the narration script to broadcast-quality MP3 audio via ElevenLabs. Send only SEGMENT_1 text (≤140 words) in the 'text' field. Returns base64-encoded audio in the 'audio' or 'audio_base64' field.

text.description: Text to convert to speech (max 140 words / ~160 chars safety limit)

data.label: ElevenLabs TTS: Segment 2
data.description: Convert SEGMENT_2 of the narration script to broadcast-quality MP3 audio via ElevenLabs. Send only SEGMENT_2 text (≤140 words) in the 'text' field. Returns base64-encoded audio in the 'audio' or 'audio_base64' field.

text.description: Text to convert to speech (max 140 words / ~160 chars safety limit)

data.label: Save Audio, Seen-Log & HTML Report
data.instruction:
Finalize the briefing:

1. AUDIO: Extract base64 audio from TTS responses. Decode both segments and concatenate into one file at ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.mp3. Play with: afplay ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.mp3
   If TTS failed, fall back to: say -v Samantha -r 175 -o ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.aiff '{combined script}'

2. SEEN-LOG: Load ~/.local/share/tech-pulse/seen-log.json (or [] if missing). Remove entries older than 14 days. Add each surfaced signal as {id: URL, title, date_seen: today}. Write back.

3. HTML REPORT: Write a self-contained HTML report to ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.html with:
   - Dark/light card layout, grouped by category (YC & Funding / Techniques / Ecosystem Shifts)
   - Each card: tag chip, title, 2-sentence description, italic 'Signal:' line explaining what it means for where things are heading, clickable source buttons with actual URLs
   - Footer: 'Generated by /tech-pulse · Seen-log: 14-day rolling window'
   Then: open ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.html

4. CONFIRM to user: audio path + report path + cost summary (~$0.06 USDC).

data.label: Tech Pulse Briefing
data.description: Text briefing (displayed inline) + audio file at ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.mp3 + HTML report at ~/Downloads/tech-pulse-{YYYYMMDD}.html. The briefing covers 5–8 signals ranked by strength. Seen-log updated for 14-day dedup.
